dependencies.description: Each node has exactly 2 ring ports forming a closed ring. Break in the ring topology prevents ERPS from functioning.
dependencies.name: Physical Ethernet ring topology

dependencies.description: Used for signal fail detection via CCM (Continuity Check Message) loss. CFM MEPs must be configured on ring ports.
dependencies.name: CFM (IEEE 802.1ag)

dependencies.description: R-APS control channel VLAN must be configured identically on all ring nodes and allowed on all ring ports.
dependencies.name: Consistent R-APS VLAN on all ring nodes

dependencies.description: One node must be designated as RPL owner (blocks RPL in IDLE state) and its adjacent neighbor as RPL neighbor.
dependencies.name: Exactly one RPL owner and one RPL neighbor per ring instance

failure_modes.description: Ring stays in PROTECTION state after the physical fault is repaired. Traffic continues taking suboptimal path around the ring instead of reverting to normal topology.

resolutions.action: Check if revertive mode is enabled on the RPL owner
Check if revertive mode is enabled on the RPL owner
Check if revertive mode is enabled on the RPL owner
Verify WTR timer hasn't expired yet — wait for full WTR duration
Verify WTR timer hasn't expired yet — wait for full WTR duration
Verify WTR timer hasn't expired yet — wait for full WTR duration
Verify RPL owner and RPL neighbor are correctly configured and adjacent to each other
Verify RPL owner and RPL neighbor are correctly configured and adjacent to each other
Verify RPL owner and RPL neighbor are correctly configured and adjacent to each other
Ensure R-APS VLAN is configured on all ring ports on all nodes
Ensure R-APS VLAN is configured on all ring ports on all nodes
Ensure R-APS VLAN is configured on all ring ports on all nodes
Verify CFM/CCM is operational on the repaired link: 'show ethernet cfm maintenance-points local'
Verify CFM/CCM is operational on the repaired link: 'show ethernet cfm maintenance-points local'
Verify CFM/CCM is operational on the repaired link: 'show ethernet cfm maintenance-points local'

root_causes.cause: Non-revertive mode configured (by design — no automatic reversion)
WTR timer still running (wait for it to expire before diagnosing)
RPL owner/neighbor mismatch — incorrect node configured as RPL owner
R-APS VLAN not passing on all ring links (pruned or blocked)
CCM (Continuity Check Messages) not recovering after physical repair

failure_modes.scenario: Ring not recovering after fault clears
symptoms: Ring stays in PROTECTION state after physical fault is repaired
Traffic taking suboptimal path through the ring
R-APS messages still showing SF or NR without RB flag

Immediately force-switch or shut ring port to stop the loop while correcting configuration

root_causes.cause: More than one node configured as RPL owner (must be exactly one per ring instance)
Misconfigured ring after node addition or replacement
Configuration copy/paste error during ring deployment

failure_modes.description: G.8032v2 sub-ring traffic is not switching on fault. Interconnection node is not propagating R-APS or topology change notifications between major ring and sub-ring.

resolutions.action: Verify sub-ring configuration including virtual channel and TC (topology change) propagation on interconnection nodes

Ensure interconnection nodes are properly configured with both major ring and sub-ring instances

Check that sub-ring instance ID is unique and does not conflict with major ring instance
Check that sub-ring instance ID is unique and does not conflict with major ring instance

root_causes.cause: Sub-ring not configured with correct virtual channel
Interconnection node not configured for R-APS propagation (topology change propagation disabled)
Sub-ring R-APS VLAN not matching or not carried on interconnection links
Sub-ring instance ID conflict with major ring

symptoms: Sub-ring traffic not switching on fault
Interconnection node not propagating R-APS between rings
Sub-ring stays in failed state while major ring is healthy
Traffic black-hole on sub-ring protected VLANs

failure_modes.description: After a ring protection switch event, MAC addresses are not flushed. Traffic is black-holed for up to 300 seconds (MAC aging time) because stale MAC entries point to the old path.

resolutions.action: Verify FDB flush is enabled for the ring instance
Verify FDB flush is enabled for the ring instance
Verify FDB flush is enabled for the ring instance
Check guard timer setting — reduce if too long (default 500ms)
Check guard timer setting — reduce if too long (default 500ms)
Check guard timer setting — reduce if too long (default 500ms)
Manually clear MAC address table as immediate workaround: 'clear mac address-table dynamic'
Manually clear MAC address table as immediate workaround: 'clear mac address-table dynamic'
Manually clear MAC address table as immediate workaround: 'clear mac address-table dynamic'
Verify R-APS VLAN is correctly configured so flush events are received by all nodes
Verify R-APS VLAN is correctly configured so flush events are received by all nodes
Verify R-APS VLAN is correctly configured so flush events are received by all nodes

root_causes.cause: FDB flush not triggered — R-APS event not processed (DNF flag incorrectly set)
Flush logic misconfigured — per-ring vs global flush behavior
Guard timer too long, suppressing R-APS processing and flush
Node not processing R-APS(Event) messages due to VLAN mismatch

failure_modes.scenario: FDB not flushing after protection switch
symptoms: Traffic black-hole for up to 300s (MAC aging) after ring switch
Stale MAC entries pointing to old ring path

R-APS VLAN pruned or blocked by STP on a ring port (ERPS and STP should not run on same VLANs)

R-APS VLAN not allowed on intermediate trunk links between ring nodes

messages.description: No Request. Ring is healthy, all clear. Sent periodically by nodes in IDLE state.
messages.name: R-APS (NR)
messages.when_sent: Periodically in IDLE state (no fault, no request)

messages.description: Signal Fail. Link failure detected. Triggers protection switching on all ring nodes.
messages.name: R-APS (SF)
messages.when_sent: When a ring node detects signal fail (link down or CCM loss) on a ring port

messages.description: No Request, RPL Blocked. Sent by RPL owner when ring is in IDLE (normal) state. Indicates RPL is blocked and ring is healthy.
messages.name: R-APS (NR,RB)
messages.when_sent: Periodically by RPL owner in IDLE state to signal that RPL is blocked

messages.description: Manual Switch request. Operator-initiated switching to block a specific ring port.
messages.name: R-APS (MS)
messages.when_sent: When operator issues manual switch command

messages.description: Forced Switch request. Highest priority switching request. Overrides all other conditions including existing SF.
messages.name: R-APS (FS)
messages.when_sent: When operator issues forced switch command

messages.description: Flush FDB notification. Nodes receiving this flush their MAC tables for the affected ring instance to force relearning on the new topology.
messages.name: R-APS (Event)
messages.when_sent: During topology changes — sent alongside SF, NR, or other R-APS messages with the DNF flag cleared

purpose: Sub-50ms Ethernet ring protection switching with deterministic failover

states.description: Ring is healthy. RPL (Ring Protection Link) is blocked by the RPL owner, all other ring ports are forwarding. Steady state — traffic flows on the shortest path around the ring, avoiding the RPL.
states.name: IDLE

states.description: Signal fail detected on a ring link. RPL is unblocked by the RPL owner, and the failed link is blocked. Traffic is rerouted around the ring in the opposite direction. FDB is flushed.
states.name: PROTECTION

states.description: Manual switch request issued by operator. RPL is unblocked, and the requested port is blocked. Behaves similarly to PROTECTION but triggered administratively.
states.name: MANUAL_SWITCH

states.description: Forced switch request issued by operator. Overrides all other conditions including existing signal fail. Highest priority switching request.
states.name: FORCED_SWITCH

states.description: Waiting for WTR (Wait-to-Restore) timer after fault clears before reverting to IDLE. Only applies in revertive mode. During this period, the ring remains in protection topology.
states.name: PENDING

timers.description: Time to wait after fault clears before reverting to IDLE. Only applies in revertive mode.
timers.name: WTR (Wait-to-Restore)
timers.on_expiry: Ring reverts to IDLE, RPL re-blocked
timers.tuning_advice: Prevents flapping on intermittent faults. Shorter = faster reversion but more risk of flap. 5min is the standard value per ITU-T recommendation. Only applies in revertive mode.

timers.description: Guard timer for manual/forced switch operations. Prevents acting on stale R-APS messages during operator-initiated topology changes.
timers.name: WTB (Wait-to-Block)

timers.description: Prevents acting on outdated R-APS messages during topology change. After a topology event, the node ignores incoming R-APS messages until the guard timer expires.
timers.name: Guard Timer
timers.tuning_advice: Must be long enough for old R-APS messages to flush from ring but short enough for fast recovery. 500ms is the ITU-T default and works for most ring sizes.

timers.description: Delay before reporting SF to ERPS. Allows underlying transport protection (e.g., SONET/SDH) to act first before triggering ring-level protection switching.
timers.name: Hold-off Timer
timers.on_expiry: SF reported to ERPS, protection switching triggered
timers.tuning_advice: Set to underlying protection switch time + margin. E.g., if SONET has 50ms protection, set hold-off to 100ms. Leave at 0ms when no underlying transport protection exists.

transitions.trigger: Signal fail (SF) detected on ring link — R-APS(SF) sent, RPL owner unblocks RPL port, other nodes flush FDB
Signal fail clears — WTR timer starts (revertive mode) or stays in PROTECTION (non-revertive)
WTR timer expires — R-APS(NR,RB) sent, RPL re-blocked, FDB flush, ring reverts to normal
Manual switch command issued — similar to SF behavior
Forced switch command (overrides even existing SF)

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Intermittent fault causing repeated SF/clear cycles; consider increasing hold-off timer
transitions.trigger: New signal fail during WTR period — WTR cancelled, back to PROTECTION

notes.note: IOS-XE supports G.8032 ERPSv2 on ASR 920, ASR 903, Catalyst 8000. Configured under 'ethernet ring g8032 <name>'. CFM required for fault detection. 'show ethernet ring g8032' commands. Common in SP access/aggregation networks.
IOS-XR supports G.8032 on ASR 9000, NCS 5500. Configured under 'ethernet ring g8032 <name>'. Full v2 support with sub-rings, multiple instances. 'show ethernet ring g8032' for ring state.
NX-OS has limited G.8032 support — available on Nexus 7000 series only. Not supported on Nexus 9000/3000. 'feature erps' required. Most NX-OS deployments use vPC or STP instead of ring protection.

G.8032 ERPS is the primary L2 protection mechanism on SAOS — preferred over STP/RSTP for all carrier Ethernet ring deployments. Supports sub-50ms switchover. Full v2 with sub-rings and multiple instances. Hardware-assisted on all platforms. Integrated with CFM for fault detection (3.3ms/10ms CCM). Subject-verb CLI: 'ring-protection show' not 'show ethernet ring'. Supports both revertive and non-revertive modes. RPL owner, RPL neighbor, and interconnection node roles.
